python-internals.title: Understanding the Python Global Interpreter Lock (GIL)
python-internals.description: The Global Interpreter Lock (GIL) is a mutex in CPython (the standard Python interpreter) that allows only one native thread to execute Python bytecode at a time, even on multi-core processors.

python-internals.title: The Secret Life of Python: Understanding the GIL
python-internals.description: Back in the day, to keep Python's internal memory management simple and thread-safe, the creators decided it was easier to just lock the whole interpreter.

python-internals.title: Python - Python Internals: Memory Management & the Global Interpreter Lock (GIL)
python-internals.description: This topic explains how Python works behind the scenes to manage memory and execute code efficiently.

python-internals.title: What is the Global Interpreter Lock (GIL) in Python? Is It Going Away in 2026?
python-internals.description: Learn what Python's Global Interpreter Lock (GIL) is, why it slows multi-threaded code, and whether it's being removed in 2026.

python-internals.title: Python's GIL Is Gone — What That Actually Means for Your Code
python-internals.description: The Global Interpreter Lock (GIL) is a mutex that protects Python's internal memory management. Only one thread can execute Python bytecode at a time.

python-internals.title: The Python GIL Controversy: Why Multi-Core Parallelism Remains Broken and Why It Might Not Matter
python-internals.description: Python uses reference counting for memory management—each object tracks how many references point to it. When that count hits zero, the object gets deleted.

python-internals.title: Understanding Python GIL (Global Interpreter Lock)
python-internals.description: While the GIL simplifies memory management and improves performance in single-threaded programs, it can be a bottleneck for multi-threaded programs.

python-internals.title: Python Complete Guide | CPython Internals: GIL
python-internals.description: The GIL is a mutex that ensures only one thread runs Python bytecode at a time in a standard CPython process.

python-internals.title: Taming the GIL: Understanding and Mitigating Python's Global Interpreter Lock
python-internals.description: While often perceived as a limitation, understanding the GIL is crucial for writing efficient and performant Python code.

python-internals.title: What is the Python Global Interpreter Lock (GIL)
python-internals.description: The Global Interpreter Lock (GIL) is a mutex used in the CPython interpreter. It ensures that only one thread executes Python bytecode at a time.
